General Symptoms.Yellow Leaves: Usually Nitrogen deficiency. Apply compost or diluted cow urine.
General Symptoms.White Powder on Leaves: Powdery Mildew. Spray 1 part milk to 9 parts water.
General Symptoms.Holes in Leaves: Caterpillars. Hand-pick or use Neem Seed Kernel Extract.
General Symptoms.Wilting Plant: Root Rot or Wilt. Check drainage; apply Trichoderma to roots.
General Symptoms.Curled Leaves: Sucking pests (Aphids/Thrips). Use yellow sticky traps and Neem oil.
General Symptoms.Fruit Dropping: Boron deficiency or water stress. Apply Borax and water regularly.
General Symptoms.Stunted Growth: Zinc deficiency. Apply 25kg/ha Zinc Sulfate to the soil.

Rice (Paddy).1. Blast: Spray fermented curd or salt water. Use resistant varieties like IR64.
Rice (Paddy).2. Bacterial Blight: Drain the field for 48 hours. Avoid excess urea.
Rice (Paddy).3. Brown Plant Hopper: Maintain 'alleyways' for sunlight; use Neem-based sprays.
Rice (Paddy).4. Stem Borer: Install light traps at night. Clip leaf tips before transplanting.
Rice (Paddy).5. Leaf Folder: Run a rope over the crop to dislodge larvae from folded leaves.
Rice (Paddy).6. Gall Midge: Apply neem cake to soil. Use light traps to catch adult flies.
Rice (Paddy).7. Rice Hispa: Manual picking of beetles. Flood the field to drown larvae.
Rice (Paddy).8. Khaira Disease: Zinc deficiency. Spray 5kg Zinc Sulfate with 2.5kg Lime.
Rice (Paddy).9. Tungro Virus: Control Green Leaf Hoppers using yellow sticky traps.
Rice (Paddy).10. Sheath Blight: Avoid excessive Nitrogen. Spray soil with Trichoderma viride.
Rice (Paddy).11. Swarming Caterpillar: Flood the nursery so pests float to the surface for birds.
Rice (Paddy).12. False Smut: Spray 0.1% Carbendazim at the booting stage.

Wheat.1. Yellow Rust: Spray sour buttermilk (diluted) or remove infected plants.
Wheat.2. Loose Smut: Soak seeds in water, then dry in hot sun before sowing.
Wheat.3. Termites: Apply neem cake. Avoid using half-decomposed cow dung.
Wheat.4. Powdery Mildew: Spray Wettable Sulfur (2g/liter) or Neem oil.
Wheat.5. Aphids: Spray Garlic, Ginger, and Chilli extract mixture.
Wheat.6. Karnal Bunt: Avoid excessive irrigation during flowering stage.
Wheat.7. Brown Rust: Sow seeds early in the season to escape peak heat.
Wheat.8. Stem Rust: Remove weeds like 'Barberry' near the fields.
Wheat.9. Root Rot: Seed treatment with Trichoderma (4g per kg of seed).
Wheat.10. Ear Cockle: Float seeds in 5% salt water; remove the floating galls.
Wheat.11. Pink Borer: Use light traps and rotate with non-cereal crops.
Wheat.12. Flag Smut: Avoid deep sowing and practice 3-year crop rotation.

Sugarcane.1. Red Rot: Use healthy setts. Smear ends with burnt lime.
Sugarcane.2. Whip Smut: Remove smutted whips in a bag and burn them.
Sugarcane.3. Early Shoot Borer: Do 'earthing up' (piling soil around base) at 45 days.
Sugarcane.4. Top Borer: Collect and destroy egg masses on the leaves.
Sugarcane.6. Grassy Shoot: Treat setts with hot water (50°C) for 2 hours.
Sugarcane.7. Mealybugs: Remove lower dry leaves (detrashing) to expose bugs.
Sugarcane.8. Whitefly: Avoid nitrogen overdose. Ensure proper drainage.
Sugarcane.9. Red Ant: Apply tobacco waste or neem cake around the base.
Sugarcane.10. Wilt: Select resistant varieties; avoid waterlogged fields.
Sugarcane.11. Root Borer: Deep plowing after harvest to expose larvae to sun.
Sugarcane.12. Scaly Insect: Spray fish oil rosin soap or neem oil.

Cotton.1. Pink Bollworm: Use Pheromone traps (5 per acre). Destroy crop residue.
Cotton.2. Whitefly: Use yellow sticky traps. Spray Neem Seed Kernel Extract.
Cotton.3. Aphids: Spray 5% Neem oil or cold water forcefully.
Cotton.4. Jassids: Apply Neem oil or castor oil on leaf undersides.
Cotton.5. Leaf Curl Virus: Uproot infected plants; control whitefly population.
Cotton.6. Black Arm: Treat seeds with Cow Dung slurry or Streptocycline.
Cotton.7. Grey Mildew: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture or Sulfur dust.
Cotton.8. Red Leaf Spot: Apply Magnesium Sulfate to correct deficiency.
Cotton.9. Boll Rot: Ensure proper spacing to allow sunlight inside canopy.
Cotton.10. Thrips: Use blue sticky traps; avoid excessive urea.
Cotton.11. Mealybug: Spray soap water mixed with kerosene (very small amount).
Cotton.12. Tobacco Caterpillar: Hand-pick larvae and eggs from leaves.

Tomato.1. Early Blight: Remove lower leaves near soil. Spray baking soda.
Tomato.2. Leaf Curl: Control Whiteflies with yellow sticky traps.
Tomato.3. Fruit Borer: Plant Marigold as a trap crop every 15 rows.
Tomato.4. Bacterial Wilt: Avoid planting where Peppers or Potatoes grew.
Tomato.5. Blossom End Rot: Calcium deficiency. Add crushed eggshells to soil.
Tomato.6. Damping Off: Improve nursery drainage; do not overwater.
Tomato.7. Red Spider Mites: Spray water forcefully on undersides of leaves.
Tomato.9. Septoria Spot: Mulch soil to prevent fungus from splashing up.
Tomato.10. Anthracnose: Avoid overhead watering; remove infected fruits.
Tomato.11. Pinworm: Use pheromone traps and rotate with non-solanaceous crops.
Tomato.12. Fusarial Wilt: Apply Trichoderma viride to soil before sowing.

Maize (Corn).1. Fall Armyworm: Apply sand or wood ash into the plant whorls.
Maize (Corn).2. Downy Mildew: Ensure good drainage; remove infected plants.
Maize (Corn).4. Leaf Blight: Apply composted manure to strengthen plant immunity.
Maize (Corn).5. Cob Rot: Harvest as soon as mature; dry cobs properly.
Maize (Corn).6. Aphids: Encourage ladybird beetles; spray neem extract.
Maize (Corn).7. Pink Borer: Deep summer plowing to kill hibernating larvae.
Maize (Corn).8. Banded Leaf/Sheath: Strip lower 2-3 leaves to prevent spread.
Maize (Corn).9. Maize Smut: Remove and burn galls before they burst open.
Maize (Corn).10. Zinc Deficiency: White stripes on leaves. Apply Zinc Sulfate.
Maize (Corn).11. Grasshoppers: Tilling soil in summer to destroy egg pods.
Maize (Corn).12. Termites: Apply neem oil emulsion in irrigation water.

Chilli.1. Leaf Curl: Spray 5% Neem oil to control mites and thrips.
Chilli.2. Die Back: Prune infected twigs and spray 1% Bordeaux mixture.
Chilli.3. Fruit Rot: Select disease-free seeds; avoid excess moisture.
Chilli.4. Thrips: Use silver mulch or intercrop with onions.
Chilli.5. Mites: Spray wettable sulfur or forcefully wash with water.
Chilli.6. Powdery Mildew: Mix 1 part milk with 9 parts water and spray.
Chilli.7. Bacterial Wilt: Plant in raised beds to prevent water stagnation.
Chilli.8. Aphids: Use yellow sticky traps and soap water spray.
Chilli.9. Root Rot: Treat seeds with Trichoderma viride powder.
Chilli.11. Pod Borer: Spray Neem Seed Kernel Extract (NSKE) at 5%.
Chilli.12. Damping Off: Treat nursery bed with wood ash and sand.

Potato.1. Late Blight: Spray ginger-garlic extract or baking soda.
Potato.2. Early Blight: Crop rotation and removal of old plant debris.
Potato.3. Common Scab: Maintain high soil moisture during tuber formation.
Potato.5. Black Scurf: Treat tubers with 3% Boric Acid before planting.
Potato.6. Bacterial Wilt: Use only certified disease-free seed tubers.
Potato.8. Cutworms: Flood the field to bring larvae to surface for birds.
Potato.10. Hollow Heart: Avoid excessive watering and sudden fertilizer bursts.
Potato.12. Root-Knot: Plant Marigold or Mustard before Potato crop.

Mango.1. Powdery Mildew: Spray Wettable Sulfur or Neem oil.
Mango.2. Mango Hopper: Prune branches to allow sunlight; use Neem spray.
Mango.3. Anthracnose: Remove dead twigs; spray Copper Oxychloride.
Mango.4. Fruit Fly: Use Tulsi/Basil traps or Pheromone traps.
Mango.5. Mealybug: Apply 40cm wide plastic band around the trunk base.
Mango.6. Malformation: Prune diseased shoots and spray 200ppm NAA.
Mango.7. Die Back: Clean pruning cuts and apply Bordeaux paste.
Mango.8. Stem Borer: Clean the hole and plug with cotton soaked in kerosene.
Mango.9. Black Tip: Caused by brick kiln fumes. Spray Borax (0.6%).
Mango.10. Gummosis: Improve drainage; scrape bark and apply Bordeaux.
Mango.11. Stone Weevil: Collect and burn fallen fruits immediately.
Mango.12. Sooty Mold: Control hoppers; spray starch solution to peel off mold.

Mustard.1. Aphids: Spray 5% NSKE or soap water in the evening.
Mustard.2. White Rust: Avoid late sowing; destroy crop residues.
Mustard.3. Alternaria Blight: Seed treatment with hot water (50°C) for 20 mins.
Mustard.4. Downy Mildew: Maintain proper spacing; use resistant seeds.
Mustard.5. Painted Bug: Irrigate the field to drown the bugs.
Mustard.6. Mustard Sawfly: Hand-pick larvae; summer plowing to expose pupae.
Mustard.7. Powdery Mildew: Dust with Sulfur or spray Neem oil.
Mustard.8. Sclerotinia Rot: Clean seeds from sclerotia; long crop rotation.
Mustard.9. Leaf Miner: Remove infested leaves manually during early stage.
Mustard.10. Bihar Hairy Caterpillar: Use light traps and hand-pick egg masses.
Mustard.11. Root Rot: Avoid waterlogging; use Trichoderma seed treatment.
Mustard.12. Sulfur Deficiency: Yellowing of young leaves. Apply Gypsum.

Onion.1. Purple Blotch: Spray mancozeb or use 2-year crop rotation.
Onion.2. Thrips: Use silver/white plastic mulch or sprinkle water forcefully.
Onion.3. Basal Rot: Treat seeds with Trichoderma viride; avoid field injury.
Onion.4. Downy Mildew: Wide spacing for airflow; avoid overhead irrigation.
Onion.5. Onion Maggot: Apply neem cake to soil; rotate with non-bulb crops.
Onion.6. Stemphylium Blight: Remove old crop residues; spray 1% Bordeaux mixture.
Onion.7. White Rot: Increase soil pH by adding lime; use clean seedlings.
Onion.8. Neck Rot: Dry onions thoroughly in the sun before storing.
Onion.9. Smut: Deep summer plowing and seed treatment with Thiram.
Onion.10. Zinc Deficiency: Yellowing/twisting of leaves. Apply Zinc Sulfate.
Onion.11. Black Mold: Store in cool, dry, well-ventilated areas.
Onion.12. Head Smut: Remove and burn infected flower heads immediately.

Apple.1. Apple Scab: Remove fallen leaves in winter. Spray 5% Urea on floor.
Apple.2. Woolly Aphid: Use resistant rootstocks (M7, M111). Release parasitic wasps.
Apple.3. San Jose Scale: Spray 2% Winter Oil Emulsion during the dormant stage.
Apple.4. Powdery Mildew: Prune infected silver-gray twigs in winter.
Apple.5. Fire Blight: Prune branches 12 inches below the visible infection.
Apple.6. Canker: Clean the wound and apply Bordeaux paste (Copper + Lime).
Apple.7. Root Rot: Ensure perfect drainage; do not let water stand at base.
Apple.8. Alternaria Blight: Ensure balanced fertilizer; avoid excessive Nitrogen.
Apple.9. Mites: Spray water or Neem oil on the underside of leaves.
Apple.11. Codling Moth: Use pheromone traps and wrap trunks with corrugated paper.
Apple.12. Crown Gall: Avoid wounding the roots during planting or tilling.

Chickpea (Chana).1. Wilt: Practice 3-year crop rotation; avoid late sowing.
Chickpea (Chana).2. Pod Borer: Install 'T' shaped bird perches to attract insect-eaters.
Chickpea (Chana).3. Ascochyta Blight: Use disease-free seeds; remove crop debris.
Chickpea (Chana).4. Root Rot: Treat seeds with Trichoderma; avoid waterlogged soil.
Chickpea (Chana).5. Botrytis Grey Mold: Reduce seed rate to allow more air between plants.
Chickpea (Chana).6. Cutworms: Deep plowing in summer to expose larvae to birds.
Chickpea (Chana).8. Rust: Early sowing helps the crop mature before rust peak.
Chickpea (Chana).9. Collar Rot: Select well-drained fields; avoid deep sowing.
Chickpea (Chana).10. Stunt Virus: Control leafhoppers; remove weed hosts near field.
Chickpea (Chana).11. Iron Deficiency: Yellowing of top leaves. Spray 0.5% Ferrous Sulfate.
Chickpea (Chana).12. Termites: Apply Neem cake or well-rotted manure only.

Banana.1. Panama Wilt: Apply lime to soil; avoid suckers from infected areas.
Banana.2. Bunchy Top Virus: Inject kerosene (4ml) into the infected pseudostem.
Banana.3. Sigatoka Leaf Spot: Remove spotted leaves; ensure wide spacing (1.8m).
Banana.4. Pseudostem Borer: Clean the plant base; use split-stem traps.
Banana.5. Rhizome Weevil: Dip suckers in clay slurry mixed with Neem oil.
Banana.6. Fruit Scarring Beetle: Remove the 'male bud' after fruit set is complete.
Banana.7. Heart Rot: Improve drainage; avoid planting during heavy rains.
Banana.8. Cigar End Rot: Remove pistils (flower ends) by hand after fruit set.
Banana.9. Nematodes: Paring (trimming) and burning of infected rhizome parts.
Banana.10. Potassium Deficiency: Orange/yellow margins on old leaves. Apply MOP.
Banana.11. Aphids: Spray soap water or Neem oil on the central leaf.
Banana.12. Erwinia Rot: Select only healthy, certified suckers for planting.

Citrus (Lemon/Orange).1. Citrus Canker: Prune infected twigs; spray 1% Bordeaux mixture.
Citrus (Lemon/Orange).2. Citrus Psylla: Control using Neem oil spray during new flush.
Citrus (Lemon/Orange).3. Leaf Miner: Spray Neem oil (5ml/L) when new leaves appear.
Citrus (Lemon/Orange).4. Gummosis: Scrape the oozing gum and apply Bordeaux paste.
Citrus (Lemon/Orange).5. Tristeza Virus: Use resistant rootstocks (Rangpur Lime). Control aphids.
Citrus (Lemon/Orange).6. Fruit Sucking Moth: Dispose of fallen/rotting fruits. Use light traps.
Citrus (Lemon/Orange).7. Die Back: Apply Zinc and Copper sprays; prune dead wood.
Citrus (Lemon/Orange).8. Mealybugs: Release Ladybird beetles or spray soap emulsion.
Citrus (Lemon/Orange).9. Lemon Butterfly: Hand-pick green caterpillars and egg masses.
Citrus (Lemon/Orange).10. Green Grubs: Release parasitic wasps; avoid chemical overuse.
Citrus (Lemon/Orange).11. Zinc Deficiency: Small, chlorotic leaves. Spray Zinc Sulfate.

Coffee.1. Coffee Leaf Rust: Prune for air circulation; spray 0.5% Bordeaux mix.
Coffee.2. White Stem Borer: Scrub the bark of main stems to remove eggs.
Coffee.3. Coffee Berry Borer: Pick and burn 'mummy berries' left on the ground.
Coffee.4. Black Rot: Thin out the shade trees to allow more sunlight.
Coffee.5. Mealybugs: Release Cryptolaemus beetles; use sticky bands.
Coffee.6. Green Scale: Encourage fungus 'Lecanicillium' by keeping humidity high.
Coffee.7. Shot Hole Borer: Prune and burn affected twigs immediately.
Coffee.8. Root Diseases: Uproot infected trees including all roots and burn.
Coffee.9. Leaf Spot: Ensure balanced nutrition; avoid overhead irrigation.
Coffee.11. Anthracnose: Apply Copper Oxychloride before the monsoon starts.
Coffee.12. Nitrogen Deficiency: General yellowing. Apply compost or Urea.

Cardamom.1. Katte Virus: Uproot and burn infected clumps. Control aphids.
Cardamom.2. Thrips: Maintain 50-60% shade. Spray Neem oil regularly.
Cardamom.3. Capsule Rot: Ensure proper drainage; remove rotting mulch.
Cardamom.4. Rhizome Rot: Treat soil with Trichoderma viride enriched manure.
Cardamom.5. Shoot Borer: Prune and destroy bored shoots in early stages.
Cardamom.6. Root Borer: Drench the soil with Neem-based pesticides.
Cardamom.7. Chenthal (Leaf Spot): Remove infected leaves; spray 1% Bordeaux mix.
Cardamom.8. Nematodes: Apply Neem cake (1kg per clump) twice a year.
Cardamom.9. Whitefly: Use yellow sticky traps; spray soap water.
Cardamom.10. Vein Clearing: Remove weed hosts; control insect vectors.
Cardamom.11. Leaf Blight: Avoid excessive shade; improve air movement.
Cardamom.12. Nut Cracking: Boron deficiency. Apply 10g Borax per clump.

Guava.1. Wilt: Inject 0.1% Carbendazim; apply Zinc Sulfate to roots.
Guava.2. Fruit Fly: Use Tulsi/Basil leaf traps; dispose of fallen fruit.
Guava.3. Anthracnose: Prune dead twigs; spray Copper Oxychloride.
Guava.4. Mealybug: Band the trunk with grease to stop ants.
Guava.6. Bark Eating Caterpillar: Plug holes with cotton soaked in kerosene.
Guava.7. Scab: Ensure sunlight reaches the center of the canopy.
Guava.9. Fruit Canker: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture during fruit set.
Guava.11. Root-Knot Nematode: Intercrop with Marigold; apply Neem cake.
Guava.12. Aphids: Spray forceful jet of water followed by Neem oil.

Grapes.1. Downy Mildew: Prune vines for air; spray 1% Bordeaux mixture.
Grapes.2. Powdery Mildew: Apply Wettable Sulfur (2g/L) or Neem oil.
Grapes.4. Thrips: Use blue sticky traps; spray garlic-chilli extract.
Grapes.5. Anthracnose: Remove and burn infected twigs and dried berries.
Grapes.6. Flea Beetle: Deep plowing in winter; spray Neem Seed Kernel Extract.
Grapes.7. Stem Borer: Plug holes with cotton soaked in kerosene or petrol.
Grapes.8. Pink Mealybug: Remove loose bark from the trunk to destroy hiding spots.
Grapes.9. Berry Drop: Usually caused by high humidity or Boron deficiency.
Grapes.10. Water Berry: Avoid excessive Nitrogen; ensure balanced irrigation.
Grapes.11. Rust: Apply Sulfur dust; remove infected old leaves.
Grapes.12. Bacterial Canker: Use sterilized pruning tools; spray Streptocycline.

Pomegranate.2. Fruit Borer: Bag the fruits with paper bags when they are marble-sized.
Pomegranate.3. Wilt: Apply Trichoderma viride enriched manure to the roots.
Pomegranate.4. Anthracnose: Prune dead wood; avoid overhead irrigation.
Pomegranate.5. Thrips/Mites: Spray 5% Neem oil or cold water forcefully.
Pomegranate.6. Fruit Cracking: Maintain consistent soil moisture; apply Borax.
Pomegranate.7. Sunscald: Use a thin coating of Kaolin clay on exposed fruits.
Pomegranate.8. Stem Borer: Clean the trunk and apply a mud-paste of Neem oil.
Pomegranate.9. Leaf Spot: Remove fallen leaves; spray ginger-garlic extract.
Pomegranate.10. Root-Knot Nematode: Plant Marigold in the orchard basins.
Pomegranate.11. Aphids: Release Ladybird beetles; use yellow sticky traps.
Pomegranate.12. Internal Breakdown: Harvest at right maturity; avoid over-ripening.

Papaya.1. Ring Spot Virus: Intercrop with Maize to block insect vectors.
Papaya.2. Leaf Curl: Control Whiteflies; remove and burn infected plants.
Papaya.3. Mealybug: Spray soap water mixed with 1% Neem oil.
Papaya.4. Foot Rot: Raised beds for drainage; treat with copper oxychloride.
Papaya.5. Powdery Mildew: Spray 1 part milk + 9 parts water on leaves.
Papaya.6. Anthracnose: Pick fruits before over-ripe; avoid bruising.
Papaya.7. Stem Rot: Avoid wounding the trunk during weeding/tilling.
Papaya.8. Mites: Spray wettable sulfur on the underside of leaves.
Papaya.9. Root-Knot: Apply Neem cake (2kg/tree) twice a year.
Papaya.11. Fruit Fly: Use Pheromone or Tulsi/Basil water traps.

Coconut.1. Rhinoceros Beetle: Place salt + sand mixture (1:2) in top leaf axils.
Coconut.2. Red Palm Weevil: Inject Pheromone or use Pheromone bucket traps.
Coconut.3. Bud Rot: Apply Bordeaux paste to the crown after cleaning.
Coconut.4. Eriophyid Mite: Spray 2% Neem oil-Garlic-Soap emulsion on buttons.
Coconut.6. Root Wilt: Apply 50kg compost and 5kg Neem cake per year.
Coconut.7. Tanjavur Wilt: Isolate tree with a 1m deep trench; apply Neem cake.
Coconut.8. Stem Bleeding: Chisel out infected part and apply hot Coal Tar.
Coconut.9. Leaf Rot: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture on the spindle leaf.
Coconut.11. Button Shedding: Usually caused by poor irrigation or Boron deficiency.
Coconut.12. Slug Caterpillar: Hand-pick larvae; spray 5% Neem Seed Kernel Extract.

Black Pepper.1. Quick Wilt: Apply Neem cake to roots; ensure no water stagnation in monsoon.
Black Pepper.2. Slow Wilt: Improve drainage; apply Trichoderma-enriched manure to soil.
Black Pepper.3. Pollu Beetle: Spray 5% Neem oil during the berry formation stage.
Black Pepper.4. Top Shoot Borer: Prune and burn infested terminal shoots in early stages.
Black Pepper.5. Leaf Gall Thrips: Spray 5% NSKE (Neem Seed Kernel Extract) on new leaves.
Black Pepper.6. Mealybugs: Apply grease bands on the vine base to block ant movement.
Black Pepper.7. Anthracnose: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture before and after the monsoon.
Black Pepper.8. Scale Insects: Spray fish oil rosin soap or 2% Neem oil emulsion.
Black Pepper.9. Phyllody: Stunted growth; uproot and burn infected vines immediately.
Black Pepper.10. Root-Knot Nematode: Apply 1kg Neem cake per vine annually in the basin.
Black Pepper.11. Zinc Deficiency: Small, narrow leaves; spray 0.5% Zinc Sulfate.
Black Pepper.12. Foot Rot: Remove mulch during monsoon to avoid excess humidity buildup.

Bajra (Pearl Millet).1. Ergot: Soak seeds in 10% salt water; remove floating fungus and burn.
Bajra (Pearl Millet).2. Downy Mildew: Rogue out (uproot) infected plants as soon as noticed.
Bajra (Pearl Millet).3. Smut: Remove infected ears in a plastic bag to prevent spore spread.
Bajra (Pearl Millet).4. Shoot Fly: Increase seed rate and remove dead hearts 3 weeks after sowing.
Bajra (Pearl Millet).5. Stem Borer: Pull out and destroy stubble after harvest to kill larvae.
Bajra (Pearl Millet).6. Grey Weevil: Apply neem cake to the soil during land preparation.
Bajra (Pearl Millet).7. Rust: Early sowing helps escape the peak humidity that causes rust.
Bajra (Pearl Millet).8. Blight: Ensure crop rotation with non-cereal crops for 2 years.
Bajra (Pearl Millet).9. Grasshoppers: Tilling soil in summer to destroy egg pods.
Bajra (Pearl Millet).10. Zinc Deficiency: Apply 25kg Zinc Sulfate per hectare at sowing.
Bajra (Pearl Millet).11. Birds: Use reflective ribbons or traditional scarecrows.
Bajra (Pearl Millet).12. Striga (Weed): Hand-pull the purple-flowered weed before it sets seeds.

Jowar (Sorghum).1. Grain Smut: Treat seeds with sulfur powder (4g/kg) before sowing.
Jowar (Sorghum).2. Shoot Fly: Use fish-meal traps (15 per acre) to attract and kill flies.
Jowar (Sorghum).3. Charcoal Rot: Maintain soil moisture during the grain filling stage.
Jowar (Sorghum).4. Stem Borer: Intercrop with Cowpea or Lablab bean to confuse the pest.
Jowar (Sorghum).5. Midge: Ensure all farmers in a village sow at the same time.
Jowar (Sorghum).6. Earhead Bug: Dust the ears with wood ash in the early morning.
Jowar (Sorghum).8. Anthracnose: Use clean, certified seeds; avoid waterlogging.
Jowar (Sorghum).9. Striga Weed: Apply organic mulch to suppress weed germination.
Jowar (Sorghum).10. Iron Deficiency: Top leaves turn white/yellow. Spray Ferrous Sulfate.
Jowar (Sorghum).11. Armyworm: Apply wood ash or sand into the central leaf whorl.
Jowar (Sorghum).12. Leaf Spot: Practice crop rotation with pulses or oilseeds.

Groundnut (Moongphali).1. Tikka Leaf Spot: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture or ginger-garlic extract.
Groundnut (Moongphali).2. Rust: Apply Sulfur dust (25kg/ha) in the early morning.
Groundnut (Moongphali).3. White Grub: Deep summer plowing to expose grubs to birds.
Groundnut (Moongphali).4. Bud Necrosis: Intercrop with Pearl Millet (Bajra) to act as a barrier.
Groundnut (Moongphali).6. Leaf Miner: Use light traps; rotate with Maize or Sorghum.
Groundnut (Moongphali).7. Stem Rot: Treat seeds with Trichoderma viride; avoid over-irrigation.
Groundnut (Moongphali).8. Red Hairy Caterpillar: Dig a trench around the field to stop larvae migration.
Groundnut (Moongphali).9. Pod Borer: Apply Neem cake to soil during the pegging stage.
Groundnut (Moongphali).10. Calcium Deficiency: Pods are empty (Pops). Apply Gypsum at flowering.
Groundnut (Moongphali).11. Thrips: Use yellow sticky traps and avoid water stress.
Groundnut (Moongphali).12. Rosette Virus: Control aphids and remove weed hosts near the field.

Soybean.1. Yellow Mosaic: Control Whiteflies with yellow sticky traps; uproot infected.
Soybean.2. Girdle Beetle: Remove and destroy the infested plant parts manually.
Soybean.3. Stem Fly: Apply Neem cake; ensure early sowing to avoid peak fly.
Soybean.4. Anthracnose: Seed treatment with hot water or ginger extract.
Soybean.5. Rhizoctonia Blight: Ensure proper drainage; use Trichoderma enriched manure.
Soybean.6. Tobacco Caterpillar: Hand-pick egg masses and larvae from leaves.
Soybean.7. Rust: Apply Sulfur dust; avoid planting during high humidity.
Soybean.8. Pod Blight: Use healthy seeds; follow 2-year crop rotation.
Soybean.9. Charcoal Rot: Avoid soil moisture stress during the pod-filling stage.
Soybean.10. Leaf Folder: Spray 5% Neem oil; encourage predatory wasps.
Soybean.11. Bacterial Pustule: Use resistant varieties; destroy old crop residues.
Soybean.12. Iron Chlorosis: Spray 0.5% Ferrous Sulfate on yellowing leaves.

Sunflower.1. Necrosis Virus: Grow 3 rows of Maize or Jowar as a border crop.
Sunflower.3. Alternaria Blight: Avoid overhead irrigation; use clean seeds.
Sunflower.4. Bihar Hairy Caterpillar: Hand-pick and destroy the young gregarious larvae.
Sunflower.5. Parrots/Birds: Cover the flower heads with cloth or plastic net.
Sunflower.6. Powdery Mildew: Spray Wettable Sulfur or 10% Milk solution.
Sunflower.7. Jassids: Spray Neem oil (5ml/L) on leaf undersides.
Sunflower.8. Downy Mildew: Uproot and burn infected plants; avoid waterlogging.
Sunflower.9. Seedling Blight: Treat seeds with Trichoderma; ensure proper depth.
Sunflower.10. Boron Deficiency: Head becomes hollow. Apply 2g Borax per liter water.
Sunflower.11. Rust: Remove weed hosts; use resistant hybrid varieties.
Sunflower.12. Termites: Apply Neem oil emulsion in the irrigation water.

Brinjal (Eggplant).1. Shoot & Fruit Borer: Remove and bury infested shoots/fruits weekly. Use Lucin-lure traps.
Brinjal (Eggplant).2. Little Leaf: Uproot infected plants; control leafhoppers using Neem oil spray.
Brinjal (Eggplant).3. Bacterial Wilt: Plant in raised beds; avoid fields where Potato or Chilli grew before.
Brinjal (Eggplant).4. Phomopsis Blight: Soak seeds in 45°C water for 30 mins; spray 1% Bordeaux mixture.
Brinjal (Eggplant).5. Epilachna Beetle: Hand-pick yellow spiny grubs and beetles; spray 5% NSKE.
Brinjal (Eggplant).6. Jassids: Apply Neem cake to soil; use yellow sticky traps.
Brinjal (Eggplant).7. Root-Knot Nematode: Intercrop with African Marigold; apply 2 tons/acre Neem cake.
Brinjal (Eggplant).8. Damping Off: Sterilize nursery soil with solar heat (Solarization) before sowing.
Brinjal (Eggplant).9. Spider Mites: Spray Wettable Sulfur or wash leaves with a high-pressure water jet.
Brinjal (Eggplant).10. Mosaic Virus: Control aphids; remove weed hosts like Solanum nigrum from bunds.
Brinjal (Eggplant).11. Stem Borer: Uproot and burn the stubble after final harvest.
Brinjal (Eggplant).12. Iron Deficiency: New leaves turn yellow. Spray 0.5% Ferrous Sulfate.

Okra (Bhindi).1. Yellow Vein Mosaic: Grow resistant varieties like Arka Anamika; control Whiteflies.
Okra (Bhindi).2. Fruit Borer: Set up pheromone traps; hand-pick infested fruits every 3 days.
Okra (Bhindi).3. Powdery Mildew: Spray a mixture of 10% milk and 90% water on leaves.
Okra (Bhindi).4. Jassids: Spray Neem oil (5ml/L) on the underside of leaves.
Okra (Bhindi).5. Root Rot: Avoid waterlogging; drench soil with Trichoderma viride culture.
Okra (Bhindi).6. Leaf Roller: Manually pick and destroy folded leaves containing caterpillars.
Okra (Bhindi).7. Whitefly: Use yellow sticky traps coated with castor oil.
Okra (Bhindi).8. Cercospora Leaf Spot: Remove older infected leaves; ensure wide plant spacing.
Okra (Bhindi).9. Mites: Usually occurs in dry heat. Increase frequency of light irrigation.
Okra (Bhindi).10. Mealybugs: Apply a band of grease around the main stem to block ants.
Okra (Bhindi).11. Damping Off: Treat seeds with Ginger extract or Trichoderma before sowing.
Okra (Bhindi).12. Potassium Deficiency: Burnt leaf margins. Apply wood ash or MOP fertilizer.

Cabbage & Cauliflower.1. Diamondback Moth: Intercrop 2 rows of Mustard for every 25 rows of Cabbage.
Cabbage & Cauliflower.2. Clubroot: Apply Lime to soil to increase pH; ensure excellent drainage.
Cabbage & Cauliflower.3. Black Rot: Soak seeds in 50°C water for 30 mins before sowing.
Cabbage & Cauliflower.4. Cabbage Butterfly: Hand-pick yellow egg masses and green caterpillars.
Cabbage & Cauliflower.5. Aphids: Spray fermented cow urine diluted 1:10 with water.
Cabbage & Cauliflower.6. Downy Mildew: Avoid overhead watering; remove lower leaves for airflow.
Cabbage & Cauliflower.7. Alternaria Blight: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture; use disease-free seeds.
Cabbage & Cauliflower.8. Head Rot: Avoid damage during weeding; harvest heads when firm.
Cabbage & Cauliflower.9. Boron Deficiency: Hollow stem/brown curd. Spray 2g Borax per liter of water.
Cabbage & Cauliflower.10. Leaf Webber: Spray 5% Neem Seed Kernel Extract (NSKE) in early evening.
Cabbage & Cauliflower.11. Cutworms: Deep plowing in summer; flood field to expose larvae to birds.

Ashwagandha (Medicinal).1. Seedling Wilt: Treat seeds with Trichoderma (5g/kg); avoid heavy soils.
Ashwagandha (Medicinal).2. Leaf Spot: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture or ginger-garlic extract.
Ashwagandha (Medicinal).3. Die Back: Prune affected twigs and apply copper oxychloride paste.
Ashwagandha (Medicinal).4. Mealybug: Spray 3% Neem oil; release Ladybird beetles.
Ashwagandha (Medicinal).6. Root Rot: Ensure field has a slope for drainage; avoid over-watering.
Ashwagandha (Medicinal).7. Aphids: Spray cold water forcefully or use 5% soap solution.
Ashwagandha (Medicinal).8. Mites: Spray 2g/L Wettable Sulfur during hot, dry periods.
Ashwagandha (Medicinal).10. Leaf Crinkling: Usually viral. Uproot infected plants; control sucking pests.
Ashwagandha (Medicinal).11. Root Grub: Apply Neem cake to soil during land preparation.
Ashwagandha (Medicinal).12. Damping Off: Thin out seedlings to improve air circulation in nursery.

Polyphagous Pests (General).1. Desert Locust: Dig 60cm deep trenches in front of marching hoppers; bury them.
Polyphagous Pests (General).2. Termites: Apply Neem cake or wood ash; avoid half-rotten manure.
Polyphagous Pests (General).3. White Grub: Summer plowing; install light traps (7pm-10pm) to catch beetles.
Polyphagous Pests (General).4. Red Hairy Caterpillar: Dig trenches around the field; use light traps for moths.
Polyphagous Pests (General).5. Armyworm: Apply wood ash or sand into plant whorls; use light traps.
Polyphagous Pests (General).6. Cutworms: Hand-collect larvae from soil at base of cut plants at night.
Polyphagous Pests (General).7. Mealybugs: Control ants using turmeric powder around the plant base.
Polyphagous Pests (General).8. Grasshoppers: Tilling soil in summer to destroy egg pods; use nets.
Polyphagous Pests (General).9. Snails/Slugs: Place salt or wood ash barriers; use wet gunny bag traps.
Polyphagous Pests (General).10. Rats/Rodents: Use local bamboo traps or plant 'Gliricidia' as a repellent.
Polyphagous Pests (General).11. Leaf Miner: Remove and burn infested leaves; spray 5% Neem oil.
Polyphagous Pests (General).12. Aphids (General): Encourage Ladybird beetles; spray soap water solution.

Cashew.1. Tea Mosquito Bug: Spray Neem oil (5ml/L) during flushing and flowering.
Cashew.2. Stem & Root Borer: Clean the tunnel and plug with cotton soaked in Neem oil.
Cashew.3. Leaf Miner: Spray 5% NSKE (Neem Seed Kernel Extract) on new leaves.
Cashew.4. Anthracnose: Prune dead twigs; spray 1% Bordeaux mixture before monsoon.
Cashew.5. Apple & Nut Borer: Collect and destroy all fallen infested fruits/nuts.
Cashew.6. Thrips: Maintain proper shade; spray water on the canopy.
Cashew.7. Powdery Mildew: Dust with Sulfur powder in the early morning.
Cashew.9. Die Back: Apply Bordeaux paste on pruned ends of branches.
Cashew.10. Shoot Tip Caterpillar: Prune and burn infested terminal shoots.
Cashew.11. Leaf Roller: Manually remove folded leaves containing larvae.
Cashew.12. Nitrogen Deficiency: General yellowing. Apply composted poultry manure.

Rubber.1. Abnormal Leaf Fall: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture on the entire canopy.
Rubber.2. Pink Disease: Apply Bordeaux paste on the affected bark areas.
Rubber.3. Powdery Mildew: Dust with sulfur; ensure trees have adequate spacing.
Rubber.4. Bird\u2019s Eye Spot: Ensure good nursery management; avoid excess moisture.
Rubber.5. Root Rot: Remove and burn the roots of dead or infected trees.
Rubber.6. Bark Rot (Black Thread): Clean the tapping panel and apply a fungicide paste.
Rubber.7. Leaf Spot: Usually occurs in young plants; improve air circulation.
Rubber.8. Stem Canker: Scrape the infected bark and apply copper-based paste.
Rubber.9. Mealybugs: Release Ladybird beetles near the base of the trees.
Rubber.10. Slugs/Snails: Use wood ash barriers around the base of young trees.
Rubber.11. Sunscorch: Apply lime-wash on the main trunk of young plants.
Rubber.12. Magnesium Deficiency: Yellowing between leaf veins. Apply Dolomite.

Vanilla.1. Root Rot: Ensure high organic matter and no waterlogging in the soil.
Vanilla.2. Stem Rot: Prune and burn infected parts; spray Copper Oxychloride (0.2%).
Vanilla.3. Bean Rot: Ensure proper shade; avoid damage during hand-pollination.
Vanilla.4. Mealybug: Spray soap water mixed with 1% Neem oil on vines.
Vanilla.5. Viral Mosaic: Uproot infected vines; control aphids and thrips immediately.
Vanilla.6. Leaf Spot: Ensure 50% shade is maintained; remove overcrowded leaves.
Vanilla.7. Slugs: Place wood ash or salt barriers around the support trees.
Vanilla.8. Premature Fruit Drop: Usually caused by sudden temperature spikes; mist the vines.
Vanilla.9. Shoot Tip Dieback: Maintain humidity; avoid wounding the growing tip during pruning.
Vanilla.10. Scale Insects: Spray Fish Oil Rosin Soap or 5% NSKE on the vines.
Vanilla.11. Ants: Place turmeric powder at the base of the support trees.
Vanilla.12. Nitrogen Deficiency: Apply well-decomposed leaf mold or vermicompost regularly.

Cocoa.1. Black Pod: Prune for air; remove and burn all infected black pods.
Cocoa.2. Stem Canker: Chisel out infected bark; apply 1% Bordeaux paste.
Cocoa.3. Vascular Streak Dieback: Prune branches 30cm below the visible internal streak.
Cocoa.4. Mealybugs: Release Cryptolaemus beetles; control ant movement.
Cocoa.5. Tea Mosquito Bug: Spray Neem oil (5ml/L) on young pods and shoots.
Cocoa.6. Pod Borer: Harvest pods weekly to break the insect life cycle.
Cocoa.7. Rats & Squirrels: Use live traps or coconut-based organic bait traps.
Cocoa.8. Cherelle Wilt: Thin out excess small pods to reduce plant stress.
Cocoa.9. Leaf Blight: Ensure balanced shade; spray 1% Bordeaux mixture.
Cocoa.10. Root Rot: Improve drainage; avoid planting in heavy, waterlogged clay.
Cocoa.11. Aphids: Spray forceful water jets followed by Neem oil spray.
Cocoa.12. Mirid Bug: Spray soap solution on the trunks and developing pods.

Clove.1. Leaf Rot: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture on the foliage during monsoon.
Clove.2. Die Back: Prune dead twigs; apply Copper-based fungicide paste.
Clove.3. Stem Borer: Clean the tunnel and plug with cotton soaked in Kerosene.
Clove.4. Leaf Spot: Avoid water stagnation near the root zone of the tree.
Clove.5. Scales: Spray 2% Neem oil emulsion on the leaves and stems.
Clove.6. Mealybugs: Apply grease bands on the trunk to stop ants climbing.
Clove.7. Sudden Death: Ensure proper drainage; avoid extreme drought stress.
Clove.8. Seedling Wilt: Drench nursery soil with Trichoderma viride culture.
Clove.9. Mites: Spray forceful water on the undersides of leaves frequently.
Clove.10. Nutrient Stress: Apply 50kg well-rotted compost per tree annually.
Clove.11. Termites: Apply wood ash around the base of the young tree.
Clove.12. Moss/Lichen: Scrape carefully from branches to avoid damaging the bark.

Nutmeg.1. Fruit Rot: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture during fruit set.
Nutmeg.2. Die Back: Remove dead branches; ensure proper shade.
Nutmeg.3. Leaf Spot: Improve air circulation by thinning shade trees.
Nutmeg.4. Thread Blight: Prune and burn infected leaves/twigs immediately.
Nutmeg.5. Scale Insects: Apply Fish Oil Rosin Soap or 5% NSKE.
Nutmeg.6. Shot Hole Borer: Remove dried twigs; maintain plant vigor.
Nutmeg.7. Root Rot: Check for waterlogging; apply Trichoderma to soil.
Nutmeg.11. Iron Deficiency: Top leaves turn yellow. Apply compost.
Nutmeg.12. Lichen Growth: Scrape from trunk to prevent moisture trapping.

Pineapple.1. Heart Rot: Improve drainage; use healthy suckers for planting.
Pineapple.2. Mealybug Wilt: Control ants; dip suckers in Neem oil before planting.
Pineapple.3. Fruit Rot: Harvest at right maturity; avoid wounding the fruit.
Pineapple.4. Root Rot: Plant in raised beds; avoid waterlogged clay soil.
Pineapple.5. Leaf Spot: Ensure proper spacing; avoid nitrogen overdose.
Pineapple.6. Thrips: Use blue sticky traps; maintain field sanitation.
Pineapple.7. Rats: Use bamboo traps or plant repellent shrubs around.
Pineapple.8. Sunscald: Cover fruit with straw or wrap with paper.
Pineapple.9. Nematodes: Apply 1kg Neem cake per square meter in nursery.
Pineapple.10. Potassium Deficiency: Yellow leaf tips. Apply wood ash.
Pineapple.11. Iron Deficiency: New leaves turn white. Spray Ferrous Sulfate.
Pineapple.12. Black Rot: Store harvested fruits in a cool, well-ventilated place.

Jackfruit.1. Rhizopus Fruit Rot: Remove and burn infected fruits; spray Bordeaux.
Jackfruit.2. Shoot Borer: Prune and destroy bored shoots and fruits.
Jackfruit.3. Mealybug: Spray soap water; release Ladybird beetles.
Jackfruit.4. Stem Borer: Clean the hole and plug with kerosene cotton.
Jackfruit.5. Pink Disease: Scrape affected bark and apply Bordeaux paste.
Jackfruit.7. Bark Eating Caterpillar: Keep the orchard clean; remove old bark.
Jackfruit.8. Scale Insects: Spray 2% Neem oil during the off-season.
Jackfruit.9. Fruit Drop: Usually caused by high wind or water stress.
Jackfruit.10. Spittle Bug: Spray water forcefully on the frothy masses.
Jackfruit.12. Nitrogen Deficiency: Yellow leaves. Apply 100kg compost per tree.

Pigeon Pea (Tur/Arhar).1. Wilt: Intercrop with Sorghum or Bajra; crop rotation.
Pigeon Pea (Tur/Arhar).2. Sterility Mosaic: Control mites; use resistant varieties like 'Asha'.
Pigeon Pea (Tur/Arhar).4. Phytophthora Blight: Ensure field drainage; avoid water stagnation.
Pigeon Pea (Tur/Arhar).5. Pod Fly: Apply Neem cake to soil; remove pods with holes.
Pigeon Pea (Tur/Arhar).6. Leaf Roller: Manually remove and destroy folded leaves.
Pigeon Pea (Tur/Arhar).7. Aphids: Spray soap water mixed with 5% Neem oil.
Pigeon Pea (Tur/Arhar).9. Thrips: Use yellow sticky traps; maintain field hygiene.
Pigeon Pea (Tur/Arhar).12. Phosphorus Deficiency: Purple tint on leaves. Apply DAP or compost.

Black Gram (Urad).2. Leaf Spot: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture or ginger extract.
Black Gram (Urad).3. Powdery Mildew: Dust with Sulfur powder in early morning.
Black Gram (Urad).4. Pod Borer: Use Pheromone traps; spray Neem oil.
Black Gram (Urad).5. Aphids: Spray soap water or fermented cow urine.
Black Gram (Urad).6. Thrips: Maintain soil moisture; use blue sticky traps.
Black Gram (Urad).7. Wilt: Practice crop rotation; use Trichoderma seeds.
Black Gram (Urad).8. Root Rot: Improve drainage; avoid planting in clay soil.
Black Gram (Urad).9. Hair Caterpillar: Dig trenches around field to stop migration.
Black Gram (Urad).10. Stem Fly: Apply Neem cake during land preparation.

Green Gram (Moong).1. Yellow Mosaic: Use yellow sticky traps for Whiteflies.
Green Gram (Moong).2. Cercospora Spot: Avoid overhead irrigation; use healthy seeds.
Green Gram (Moong).3. Macrophomina Blight: Ensure consistent soil moisture; seed treatment.
Green Gram (Moong).5. Aphids: Spray Garlic-Chilli extract in the evening.
Green Gram (Moong).7. Anthracnose: Deep summer plowing; remove old residue.
Green Gram (Moong).9. Wilt: Rotate with Mustard or Maize for 2 seasons.
Green Gram (Moong).10. Damping Off: Improve nursery drainage; avoid thick sowing.
Green Gram (Moong).11. Thrips: Apply Neem oil (5ml/L) on leaf undersides.

Lentil (Masoor).1. Wilt: Sow seeds early; use Trichoderma viride treatment.
Lentil (Masoor).2. Rust: Apply Sulfur dust; avoid excessive Nitrogen.
Lentil (Masoor).3. Aphids: Spray soap water or Neem oil emulsion.
Lentil (Masoor).5. Downy Mildew: Maintain proper plant spacing; avoid waterlogging.
Lentil (Masoor).6. Botrytis Grey Mold: Ensure good air circulation; remove old debris.
Lentil (Masoor).7. Root Rot: Apply well-rotted manure only; improve drainage.
Lentil (Masoor).8. Thrips: Use blue sticky traps; avoid drought stress.
Lentil (Masoor).9. Cutworm: Flood the field to expose larvae to birds.
Lentil (Masoor).10. Stem Fly: Apply Neem cake to the soil at sowing.
Lentil (Masoor).11. Mosaic Virus: Control sap-sucking insects; remove weed hosts.
Lentil (Masoor).12. Iron Deficiency: Top leaves turn yellow. Spray Ferrous Sulfate.

Mentha (Mint).1. Rust: Sucker treatment with hot water (45°C) for 5 mins.
Mentha (Mint).2. Leaf Spot: Ensure proper spacing; spray 1% Bordeaux mix.
Mentha (Mint).3. Powdery Mildew: Spray 10% Milk solution on the leaves.
Mentha (Mint).4. Termites: Apply Neem oil in the irrigation water.
Mentha (Mint).5. Aphids: Spray soap water or fermented cow urine.
Mentha (Mint).7. Wilt: Practice 3-year crop rotation; avoid heavy clay.
Mentha (Mint).8. Stolon Rot: Ensure field is well-drained and leveled.
Mentha (Mint).10. Thrips: Maintain high soil moisture; use sticky traps.
Mentha (Mint).12. Cutworms: Deep summer plowing to expose larvae to sun.

Aloe Vera.2. Leaf Spot: Improve air circulation; remove infected leaves.
Aloe Vera.3. Mealybug: Spray soap water mixed with 1% Neem oil.
Aloe Vera.4. Aphids: Spray forceful jet of water; use Neem spray.
Aloe Vera.5. Slugs/Snails: Use wood ash or salt barriers around plants.
Aloe Vera.6. Leaf Wilt: Usually caused by excessive direct sunlight or drought.
Aloe Vera.7. Anthracnose: Prune and burn infected leaf tips immediately.
Aloe Vera.8. Scale Insects: Gently scrape from leaves; apply Neem oil.
Aloe Vera.9. Bacterial Soft Rot: Avoid wounding the leaves; improve drainage.
Aloe Vera.10. Sooty Mold: Control aphids/scales; wash leaves with water.
Aloe Vera.11. Termites: Apply wood ash around the base of the plant.
Aloe Vera.12. Magnesium Deficiency: Leaf margins turn yellow. Apply Epsom salt.

Lemongrass.1. Leaf Spot: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture; remove old grass.
Lemongrass.2. Rust: Usually occurs in monsoon; ensure proper drainage.
Lemongrass.3. Red Spittle Bug: Spray water forcefully to wash off spittle.
Lemongrass.4. Thrips: Maintain high soil moisture; use blue sticky traps.
Lemongrass.6. White Grub: Summer plowing; apply Neem cake to soil.
Lemongrass.7. Termites: Apply Neem oil emulsion in irrigation channel.
Lemongrass.8. Anthracnose: Prune old/dried leaves before the monsoon.
Lemongrass.9. Iron Deficiency: New leaves turn yellow. Spray Ferrous Sulfate.
Lemongrass.10. Stem Borer: Harvest early if infestation is noticed.
Lemongrass.12. Slug Caterpillar: Hand-pick larvae; encourage birds in field.

Sesame (Til).1. Phyllody: Deformed flowers. Uproot infected; control hoppers.
Sesame (Til).3. Gall Fly: Apply Neem cake; remove and burn infested buds.
Sesame (Til).4. Root Rot: Treat seeds with Trichoderma; avoid waterlogging.
Sesame (Til).5. Alternaria Leaf Spot: Avoid nitrogen overdose; ensure crop rotation.
Sesame (Til).6. Bacterial Blight: Soak seeds in 50°C water for 20 minutes.
Sesame (Til).7. Powdery Mildew: Dust with sulfur; spray 1:10 milk-water mix.
Sesame (Til).8. Thrips: Use silver mulch; avoid drought stress.
Sesame (Til).9. Wilt: Practice 3-year crop rotation; use clean seeds.
Sesame (Til).10. Aphids: Spray fermented cow urine diluted with water.
Sesame (Til).11. Leaf Hopper: Use yellow sticky traps; spray Neem extract.
Sesame (Til).12. Sulfur Deficiency: Yellow leaves. Apply Gypsum to the soil.

Castor.2. Capsule Borer: Use light traps; spray Neem oil during podding.
Castor.3. Wilt: Practice crop rotation; use resistant varieties.
Castor.4. Leaf Spot: Ensure proper spacing; spray 1% Bordeaux mix.
Castor.5. Thrips: Use blue sticky traps; avoid water stress.
Castor.6. Jassids: Spray Neem oil (5ml/L) on leaf undersides.
Castor.7. Botrytis Grey Mold: Ensure proper drainage; avoid high humidity.
Castor.9. Whitefly: Use yellow sticky traps; spray soap water.
Castor.10. Tobacco Caterpillar: Collect egg masses and larvae manually.
Castor.11. Root Rot: Apply Neem cake to soil; avoid water stagnation.

Walnut (Akhrot).1. Codling Moth: Wrap trunks with corrugated paper to trap larvae; destroy the paper.
Walnut (Akhrot).2. Walnut Blight: Prune dead wood in winter; spray Copper-based organic fungicides.
Walnut (Akhrot).3. Anthracnose: Remove fallen leaves; ensure trees are not overcrowded for airflow.
Walnut (Akhrot).4. Aphids: Release Ladybird beetles or spray fermented cow urine (1:10 dilution).
Walnut (Akhrot).5. Scale Insects: Spray 2% Winter Oil Emulsion during the dormant winter stage.
Walnut (Akhrot).6. Walnut Rust: Apply Sulfur dust in early morning; remove infected old leaves.
Walnut (Akhrot).7. Root Rot: Ensure perfect drainage; avoid planting in heavy clay soil.
Walnut (Akhrot).8. Crown Gall: Avoid wounding the roots during tilling; use sterilized tools.
Walnut (Akhrot).9. Spider Mites: Spray forceful water jets on the underside of leaves.
Walnut (Akhrot).10. Zinc Deficiency: Small, chlorotic leaves; spray 0.5% Zinc Sulfate in spring.
Walnut (Akhrot).11. Boron Toxicity: Leaf tip burning; flush soil with excess water if possible.
Walnut (Akhrot).12. Bark Canker: Clean the wound and apply a paste of Copper and Lime.

Dragon Fruit.1. Stem Rot: Avoid over-watering; ensure soil is sandy and well-drained.
Dragon Fruit.2. Anthracnose: Prune infected stems; spray 1% Bordeaux mixture.
Dragon Fruit.3. Mealybugs: Spray soap water mixed with 1% Neem oil on the stems.
Dragon Fruit.4. Ants: Apply Turmeric powder at the base of the concrete poles.
Dragon Fruit.5. Fruit Thrips: Use blue sticky traps; avoid water stress during fruiting.
Dragon Fruit.6. Sunscald: Provide partial shade using 25% green shade nets in peak summer.
Dragon Fruit.7. Bird Damage: Use reflective ribbons or bird netting over the trellis.
Dragon Fruit.8. Soft Rot: Copper deficiency; apply Copper Oxychloride spray to the stem.
Dragon Fruit.9. Root-Knot Nematode: Apply Neem cake (500g per pole) twice a year.
Dragon Fruit.10. Snail/Slug Attack: Create a barrier of wood ash or salt around the support base.
Dragon Fruit.11. Flower Drop: Usually due to heavy rain; protect flowers with small plastic caps.
Dragon Fruit.12. Calcium Deficiency: Weak stem tips; add crushed eggshells or lime to the soil.

Arecanut (Supari).1. Fruit Rot (Koleroga): Cover bunches with 'Kotte' (polythene bags) before monsoon.
Arecanut (Supari).2. Yellow Leaf Disease: Apply balanced organic manure and improve soil drainage.
Arecanut (Supari).3. Bud Rot: Apply Bordeaux paste to the crown after removing rotted tissue.
Arecanut (Supari).4. Spindle Bug: Spray Neem oil into the leaf axils of the spindle leaf.
Arecanut (Supari).5. Inflorescence Caterpillar: Forcefully spray water or Neem extract into the flower sheath.
Arecanut (Supari).6. Root Wilt: Apply 50kg compost and 5kg Neem cake per palm annually.
Arecanut (Supari).7. Stem Bleeding: Chisel out infected part and apply hot Coal Tar or Bordeaux paste.
Arecanut (Supari).8. White Grubs: Deep summer plowing; apply Neem cake to the soil basin.
Arecanut (Supari).9. Nut Cracking: Boron deficiency; apply 10g Borax per palm in the basin.
Arecanut (Supari).10. Mites: Spray Wettable Sulfur (2g/L) during the hot dry season.
Arecanut (Supari).11. Leaf Spot: Ensure proper spacing; avoid nitrogen overdose in monsoon.
Arecanut (Supari).12. Sun Scorch: Paint the southern side of the trunk with Lime-wash.

Avocado (Butter Fruit).1. Phytophthora Root Rot: Mulch with dry leaves to encourage beneficial soil fungi.
Avocado (Butter Fruit).2. Avocado Thrips: Maintain high humidity; spray Neem oil on young fruits.
Avocado (Butter Fruit).3. Mites: Spray forceful water on the underside of leaves to dislodge pests.
Avocado (Butter Fruit).4. Anthracnose: Harvest fruits with stalks; avoid bruising during picking.
Avocado (Butter Fruit).5. Stem End Rot: Cool the fruits immediately after harvest to 5-10°C.
Avocado (Butter Fruit).6. Lace Bug: Spray soap water or Neem oil on the leaf undersides.
Avocado (Butter Fruit).7. Iron Deficiency: Yellowing of young leaves; apply Ferrous Sulfate spray.
Avocado (Butter Fruit).8. Zinc Deficiency: Rounded, small leaves; apply Zinc Sulfate to the soil.
Avocado (Butter Fruit).9. Fruit Borer: Remove and bury all infested fallen fruits 2 feet deep.
Avocado (Butter Fruit).10. Leaf Burn: Salt accumulation; deep irrigate to leach salts from the root zone.
Avocado (Butter Fruit).11. Algal Spot: Prune branches to improve light and reduce humidity in canopy.
Avocado (Butter Fruit).12. Canker: Avoid mechanical injury to trunk; apply copper paste to wounds.

Amla (Indian Gooseberry).1. Rust: Collect and burn fallen leaves to stop fungal spore cycle.
Amla (Indian Gooseberry).2. Bark Eating Caterpillar: Clean tunnels and plug with cotton soaked in Neem oil.
Amla (Indian Gooseberry).3. Fruit Borer: Spray 5% Neem Seed Kernel Extract during fruit setting.
Amla (Indian Gooseberry).4. Shoot Gall Maker: Prune the galled shoots and burn them immediately.
Amla (Indian Gooseberry).5. Internal Necrosis: Boron deficiency; spray 0.6% Borax during fruit growth.
Amla (Indian Gooseberry).6. Blue Mold: Avoid bruising fruit during harvest; store in cool area.
Amla (Indian Gooseberry).7. Lichen: Gently scrape from branches to prevent moisture trapping.
Amla (Indian Gooseberry).8. Aphids: Spray fermented cow urine diluted 1:10 with water.
Amla (Indian Gooseberry).9. Leaf Spot: Ensure proper spacing; avoid water stagnation in basins.
Amla (Indian Gooseberry).10. Damping Off: Improve nursery drainage; avoid thick sowing of seeds.
Amla (Indian Gooseberry).11. Zinc Deficiency: Small leaves; apply 20g Zinc Sulfate per tree.
Amla (Indian Gooseberry).12. Termites: Apply wood ash or Neem cake around the base of the tree.

Ber (Indian Jujube).1. Fruit Fly: Pick and bury dropped fruits; use Tulsi leaf water traps.
Ber (Indian Jujube).2. Powdery Mildew: Prune trees heavily in summer (May) to allow light entry.
Ber (Indian Jujube).3. Leaf Spot: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture after pruning in summer.
Ber (Indian Jujube).4. Fruit Rot: Maintain field sanitation; remove infected fruits from the tree.
Ber (Indian Jujube).5. Bark Eating Caterpillar: Inject Neem oil into holes and seal with wet mud.
Ber (Indian Jujube).6. Lac Insect: Prune infested twigs and destroy them to prevent spread.
Ber (Indian Jujube).7. Alternaria Blight: Avoid overhead irrigation; ensure wide plant spacing.
Ber (Indian Jujube).8. Jassids: Spray Neem oil (5ml/L) on the underside of leaves.
Ber (Indian Jujube).9. Thrips: Use yellow sticky traps; maintain consistent soil moisture.
Ber (Indian Jujube).10. Potassium Deficiency: Burnt leaf margins; apply wood ash in the tree basin.
Ber (Indian Jujube).11. Mites: Spray Wettable Sulfur during hot, dry weather spells.
Ber (Indian Jujube).12. Die Back: Apply Copper paste on the cut ends after annual pruning.

Nutmeg (Jaiphal).1. Fruit Rot: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture during fruit set and monsoon.
Nutmeg (Jaiphal).2. Die Back: Remove dead branches; ensure proper shade management.
Nutmeg (Jaiphal).3. Leaf Spot: Improve air circulation by thinning overcrowded shade trees.
Nutmeg (Jaiphal).4. Thread Blight: Prune and burn infected leaves and twigs immediately.
Nutmeg (Jaiphal).5. Scale Insects: Apply Fish Oil Rosin Soap or 5% Neem Seed Kernel Extract.
Nutmeg (Jaiphal).6. Shot Hole Borer: Remove dried twigs; maintain plant vigor with compost.
Nutmeg (Jaiphal).7. Root Rot: Check for waterlogging; apply Trichoderma viride to soil.
Nutmeg (Jaiphal).8. Premature Fruit Drop: Maintain consistent soil moisture; avoid extreme dry spells.
Nutmeg (Jaiphal).9. Mealybugs: Control ant movement using grease bands on the trunk.
Nutmeg (Jaiphal).10. Zinc Deficiency: Small leaves; spray 0.5% Zinc Sulfate on the foliage.
Nutmeg (Jaiphal).11. Iron Deficiency: Top leaves turn yellow; apply well-rotted manure to basin.
Nutmeg (Jaiphal).12. Lichen Growth: Scrape carefully from the trunk to prevent moisture trapping.

Cinnamon (Dalchini).1. Leaf Spot: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture; remove infected fallen leaves.
Cinnamon (Dalchini).2. Pink Disease: Prune affected branches; apply Bordeaux paste to cut ends.
Cinnamon (Dalchini).3. Leaf Miner: Spray 5% Neem oil on new leaf flushes in spring.
Cinnamon (Dalchini).4. Shoot Borer: Cut and burn infested shoots to destroy larvae inside.
Cinnamon (Dalchini).5. Grey Blight: Ensure proper spacing; avoid nitrogen overdose in monsoon.
Cinnamon (Dalchini).6. Thrips: Maintain high humidity; spray forceful water on leaves.
Cinnamon (Dalchini).7. Root Rot: Improve drainage; drench soil with 1% Copper Oxychloride.
Cinnamon (Dalchini).8. Bark Canker: Avoid mechanical damage during harvest; apply copper paste.
Cinnamon (Dalchini).9. Aphids: Spray fermented cow urine diluted 1:10 with water.
Cinnamon (Dalchini).10. Whitefly: Use yellow sticky traps coated with castor oil.
Cinnamon (Dalchini).11. Magnesium Deficiency: Apply Dolomite or Epsom salt (20g per tree).
Cinnamon (Dalchini).12. Termites: Apply Neem cake or wood ash around the tree base.

Kiwi Fruit.1. Phytophthora Root Rot: Plant on raised mounds; ensure excellent soil drainage.
Kiwi Fruit.2. Armoured Scales: Scrub vines with a soft brush during dormant winter.
Kiwi Fruit.3. Bacterial Blossom Blight: Avoid overhead irrigation during the flowering stage.
Kiwi Fruit.4. Botrytis Fruit Rot: Ensure proper vine pruning for maximum air circulation.
Kiwi Fruit.5. Leaf Roller: Manually pick and destroy folded leaves with larvae.
Kiwi Fruit.6. Mites: Usually occurs in dry heat; mist the vines with water.
Kiwi Fruit.7. Root-Knot Nematode: Apply 1kg Neem cake per vine during early spring.
Kiwi Fruit.8. Thrips: Use blue sticky traps; maintain field sanitation.
Kiwi Fruit.9. Crown Gall: Use sterilized pruning tools; avoid wounding the base.
Kiwi Fruit.10. Nitrogen Deficiency: Pale yellow leaves; apply well-rotted vermicompost.
Kiwi Fruit.11. Fruit Scarring: Caused by wind; use windbreaks like tall hedge plants.
Kiwi Fruit.12. Potassium Deficiency: Apply wood ash or MOP to the root zone.

Custard Apple (Sitaphal).1. Mealybug: Spray 2% Neem oil or release Ladybird beetles.
Custard Apple (Sitaphal).2. Anthracnose: Prune dead wood; spray 1% Bordeaux mixture.
Custard Apple (Sitaphal).3. Fruit Spot: Avoid overcrowding; remove and burn infected fruits.
Custard Apple (Sitaphal).4. Bacterial Wilt: Avoid water stagnation; treat soil with Trichoderma.
Custard Apple (Sitaphal).5. Fruit Fly: Use Tulsi (Basil) leaf water traps; bury fallen fruit.
Custard Apple (Sitaphal).6. Aphids: Spray soap water mixed with 5% Neem extract.
Custard Apple (Sitaphal).7. Root Rot: Improve drainage; avoid planting in low-lying areas.
Custard Apple (Sitaphal).8. Leaf Spot: Ensure proper spacing; spray ginger-garlic extract.
Custard Apple (Sitaphal).9. Scale Insects: Scrape manually or spray Fish Oil Rosin Soap.
Custard Apple (Sitaphal).10. Boron Deficiency: Fruit cracking; spray 0.2% Borax on the fruit.
Custard Apple (Sitaphal).11. Zinc Deficiency: Apply 20g Zinc Sulfate per tree in the basin.
Custard Apple (Sitaphal).12. Termites: Apply Neem cake around the base of the tree.

Saffron (Kesar).1. Corm Rot: Treat corms with 1% Bordeaux mixture before planting.
Saffron (Kesar).3. Rodents: Use local bamboo traps or plant repellent shrubs.
Saffron (Kesar).4. Thrips: Use blue sticky traps; maintain soil moisture.
Saffron (Kesar).5. Mites: Spray forceful water or Neem oil on the foliage.
Saffron (Kesar).6. Bacterial Rot: Ensure corms are dried properly before storage.
Saffron (Kesar).7. Leaf Blight: Remove and burn old leaves; ensure wide spacing.
Saffron (Kesar).8. Weed Competition: Manual weeding is essential during the flowering stage.
Saffron (Kesar).9. Rabbit Damage: Install small wire-mesh fencing around the beds.
Saffron (Kesar).10. Soil Hardening: Apply well-decomposed manure to keep soil friable.
Saffron (Kesar).11. Nutrient Stress: Apply 10 tons/ha of FYM (Manure) before planting.
Saffron (Kesar).12. Drainage Issue: Plant on raised beds to prevent corm decay.

Strawberry.1. Grey Mold: Use straw mulch to keep fruits off the damp soil.
Strawberry.2. Powdery Mildew: Spray 10% Milk-Water solution on the leaves.
Strawberry.3. Leaf Spot: Remove older leaves; avoid overhead irrigation.
Strawberry.4. Red Stele Root Rot: Plant in well-drained, raised beds; avoid clay.
Strawberry.5. Aphids: Release Ladybird beetles; use yellow sticky traps.
Strawberry.6. Slugs & Snails: Place salt or wood ash barriers around the beds.
Strawberry.7. Spider Mites: Spray water jets on the undersides of leaves.
Strawberry.9. Crown Rot: Avoid planting too deep; ensure proper drainage.
Strawberry.10. Ants: Place turmeric powder near the strawberry runners.
Strawberry.11. Nitrogen Deficiency: Reddish leaves; apply diluted fermented cow urine.
Strawberry.12. Bird Damage: Use reflective tapes or bird netting over the rows.

Turmeric.1. Rhizome Rot: Select disease-free seeds; treat with Trichoderma.
Turmeric.2. Leaf Spot: Spray ginger-garlic extract or 1% Bordeaux mix.
Turmeric.3. Shoot Borer: Prune and destroy bored shoots in early stages.
Turmeric.4. Rhizome Scale: Dip rhizomes in 0.1% Neem oil before planting.
Turmeric.5. Bacterial Wilt: Proper drainage; avoid runoff from infected fields.
Turmeric.6. Leaf Roller: Hand-pick folded leaves and destroy the larvae.
Turmeric.7. Thrips: Spray 5% Neem oil; use blue sticky traps.
Turmeric.8. White Grub: Deep summer plowing; apply Neem cake to soil.
Turmeric.9. Leaf Blotch: Ensure crop rotation; remove all old plant debris.
Turmeric.10. Storage Rot: Store rhizomes in pits covered with Neem leaves.
Turmeric.11. Iron Deficiency: Top leaves turn yellow; spray 0.5% Ferrous Sulfate.
Turmeric.12. Nematodes: Apply 1 ton/ha Neem cake during land preparation.

Ginger.1. Soft Rot: Provide drainage; treat rhizomes with hot water (50°C).
Ginger.2. Yellows (Wilt): Use healthy planting material; solarize the soil.
Ginger.3. Leaf Spot: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture during the monsoon.
Ginger.4. Shoot Borer: Watch for holes in the stem; prune affected shoots.
Ginger.5. Rhizome Weevil: Deep summer plowing to expose pupae to birds.
Ginger.7. Scale Insects: Dip rhizomes in Neem oil solution before sowing.
Ginger.8. Thrips: Sprinkle water to maintain humidity; use traps.
Ginger.9. Leaf Blight: Ensure balanced nutrition; avoid excess urea.
Ginger.10. Nutrient Stress: Apply 25 tons/ha of well-rotted farm manure.
Ginger.11. Magnesium Deficiency: Apply Epsom salt (10g/L) as a foliar spray.
Ginger.12. Stem Gall: Remove and burn infested plants to stop spread.

Stevia (Sweet Leaf).1. Root Rot: Avoid over-watering; ensure light, sandy-loam soil.
Stevia (Sweet Leaf).2. Leaf Spot: Improve air circulation; spray 1% Bordeaux mix.
Stevia (Sweet Leaf).3. Aphids: Spray forceful jet of water; use Neem oil spray.
Stevia (Sweet Leaf).4. Thrips: Maintain soil moisture; use blue sticky traps.
Stevia (Sweet Leaf).5. Whitefly: Use yellow sticky traps; spray 5% soap water.
Stevia (Sweet Leaf).6. Damping Off: Avoid thick sowing; treat seeds with wood ash.
Stevia (Sweet Leaf).7. Septoria Blight: Remove and burn older leaves near the soil.
Stevia (Sweet Leaf).9. Slugs: Use wood ash or salt barriers around the plants.
Stevia (Sweet Leaf).10. Nitrogen Deficiency: Pale leaves; apply diluted vermicompost tea.
Stevia (Sweet Leaf).11. Zinc Deficiency: Apply 10kg/ha Zinc Sulfate during land prep.
Stevia (Sweet Leaf).12. Boron Deficiency: Stem cracking; apply 2g Borax per liter water.

Pomegranate (Anar).1. Bacterial Blight: Spray Bleaching Powder (20g/10L) and 1% Bordeaux mixture.
Pomegranate (Anar).2. Fruit Borer: Bag the fruits with paper bags when they are marble-sized.
Pomegranate (Anar).3. Wilt: Drench soil with Trichoderma viride enriched manure.
Pomegranate (Anar).4. Anthracnose: Prune dead twigs; spray Copper Oxychloride (0.3%).
Pomegranate (Anar).5. Thrips: Use blue sticky traps; spray Neem oil (5ml/L) on flowers.
Pomegranate (Anar).6. Fruit Cracking: Boron deficiency; apply 10g Borax per tree and mulch soil.
Pomegranate (Anar).7. Sunscald: Use Kaolin clay spray (5%) to reflect sunlight from fruits.
Pomegranate (Anar).8. Stem Borer: Clean the hole and plug with cotton soaked in Neem oil.
Pomegranate (Anar).9. Leaf Spot: Remove fallen leaves; spray ginger-garlic extract.
Pomegranate (Anar).10. Root-Knot Nematode: Intercrop with Marigold in the tree basins.
Pomegranate (Anar).11. Aphids: Release Ladybird beetles; use yellow sticky traps.
Pomegranate (Anar).12. Internal Breakdown: Harvest at right maturity; avoid over-ripening on tree.

Jute.1. Stem Rot: Avoid waterlogging; treat seeds with wood ash before sowing.
Jute.2. Root Rot: Apply Neem cake to the soil; ensure proper crop rotation.
Jute.3. Yellow Mite: Spray forceful water jets on the underside of leaves.
Jute.4. Semi-looper: Install 'T' shaped bird perches to attract insect predators.
Jute.5. Bihar Hairy Caterpillar: Hand-pick egg masses and young gregarious larvae.
Jute.6. Indigo Caterpillar: Trench the field to prevent larvae from migrating.
Jute.7. Stem Weevil: Remove and burn infested plants during the thinning process.
Jute.8. Black Band: Avoid wounding the stem during weeding; use clean tools.
Jute.9. Seedling Blight: Treat seeds with Trichoderma (5g/kg) before sowing.
Jute.10. Hoppers: Use yellow sticky traps; spray 5% Neem Seed Kernel Extract.
Jute.11. Phosphorus Deficiency: Purple tint on leaves; apply well-rotted compost.
Jute.12. Nitrogen Stress: Yellowing of leaves; apply diluted fermented cow urine.

Rose (Gulab).1. Die Back: Prune 2 inches below the black part; apply Bordeaux paste.
Rose (Gulab).2. Black Spot: Remove infected fallen leaves; avoid overhead watering.
Rose (Gulab).3. Powdery Mildew: Spray a mixture of 10% milk and 90% water.
Rose (Gulab).4. Aphids: Spray soap water or Neem oil (3ml/L) in the evening.
Rose (Gulab).5. Thrips: Use yellow sticky traps; maintain soil moisture.
Rose (Gulab).6. Red Scale: Scrub the stems with a soft brush and soap solution.
Rose (Gulab).7. Bud Worm: Hand-pick caterpillars from the flower buds.
Rose (Gulab).8. Mites: Usually in dry weather; mist the plants with cold water.
Rose (Gulab).9. Botrytis Blight: Remove faded flowers (deadheading) to improve airflow.
Rose (Gulab).10. Iron Deficiency: New leaves turn yellow; spray 0.5% Ferrous Sulfate.
Rose (Gulab).11. Leaf Hopper: Spray forceful water or use Neem-based sprays.
Rose (Gulab).12. Termites: Apply wood ash around the base of the rose bush.

Tobacco.1. Damping Off: Raised nursery beds; treat soil with wood ash and sand.
Tobacco.2. Frog Eye Spot: Remove lower leaves; ensure wide spacing for airflow.
Tobacco.3. Tobacco Mosaic Virus: Wash hands with soap before touching plants; remove infected.
Tobacco.4. Root-Knot Nematode: Crop rotation with Marigold or cereal crops.
Tobacco.6. Tobacco Caterpillar: Use Pheromone traps; hand-pick larvae and egg masses.
Tobacco.7. Orobanche (Weed): Hand-pull the parasite weed before it sets seeds.
Tobacco.8. Black Shank: Improve drainage; avoid planting in low-lying areas.
Tobacco.9. Wilt: Apply Trichoderma enriched manure to the soil.
Tobacco.10. Leaf Curl: Control Whitefly population using yellow sticky traps.
Tobacco.11. Potassium Deficiency: Burnt leaf margins; apply wood ash to the soil.
Tobacco.12. Stem Borer: Uproot and burn infested plants immediately.

Pear (Nashpati).1. Fire Blight: Prune 12 inches below the infection; sterilize tools with alcohol.
Pear (Nashpati).2. Pear Psylla: Spray Neem oil during the early spring flush.
Pear (Nashpati).3. Scab: Remove fallen leaves in winter; spray 1% Bordeaux mixture.
Pear (Nashpati).4. Codling Moth: Wrap trunks with corrugated cardboard to trap larvae.
Pear (Nashpati).5. Aphids: Release Ladybird beetles; spray soap water.
Pear (Nashpati).6. Powdery Mildew: Prune silver-gray twigs in winter; apply Sulfur dust.
Pear (Nashpati).7. Root Rot: Ensure perfect drainage; avoid standing water at base.
Pear (Nashpati).8. San Jose Scale: Spray 2% Winter Oil Emulsion during dormancy.
Pear (Nashpati).9. Fruit Borer: Collect and bury all infested fallen fruits 2 feet deep.
Pear (Nashpati).10. Boron Deficiency: Fruit becomes bumpy/distorted; apply 5g Borax per tree.
Pear (Nashpati).11. Iron Chlorosis: Spray 0.5% Ferrous Sulfate on yellowing leaves.
Pear (Nashpati).12. Canker: Clean the wound and apply a paste of Copper and Lime.

Marigold (Genda).1. Damping Off: Avoid over-watering in nursery; use sand-mixed soil.
Marigold (Genda).2. Leaf Spot: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture; remove old infected leaves.
Marigold (Genda).3. Powdery Mildew: Mix 1 part milk with 9 parts water and spray.
Marigold (Genda).4. Red Spider Mites: Spray water forcefully on the underside of leaves.
Marigold (Genda).5. Bud Rot: Ensure proper spacing; avoid nitrogen overdose.
Marigold (Genda).6. Aphids: Spray soap water or fermented cow urine (1:10).
Marigold (Genda).7. Thrips: Use blue sticky traps; maintain soil moisture.
Marigold (Genda).8. Wilt: Avoid planting where Tomatoes or Potatoes grew before.
Marigold (Genda).9. Flower Borer: Spray 5% Neem extract; hand-pick infested buds.
Marigold (Genda).10. Slugs & Snails: Place wood ash or salt barriers around the beds.
Marigold (Genda).11. Nitrogen Deficiency: Pale leaves; apply diluted vermicompost tea.
Marigold (Genda).12. Botrytis Blight: Remove faded flowers to improve air movement.

Chickoo (Sapota).1. Leaf Webber: Prune and destroy the webbed shoots and leaves.
Chickoo (Sapota).2. Fruit Fly: Use Tulsi/Basil traps; dispose of fallen fruits.
Chickoo (Sapota).3. Mealybug: Band the trunk with grease or plastic to stop ants.
Chickoo (Sapota).5. Sooty Mold: Control scale insects/aphids; wash with starch solution.
Chickoo (Sapota).6. Scale Insects: Spray Fish Oil Rosin Soap or 2% Neem oil.
Chickoo (Sapota).7. Stem Borer: Clean the hole and plug with kerosene-soaked cotton.
Chickoo (Sapota).8. Anthracnose: Prune dead twigs; spray 1% Bordeaux mixture.
Chickoo (Sapota).9. Leaf Spot: Ensure proper spacing; remove old fallen leaves.
Chickoo (Sapota).10. Zinc Deficiency: Small leaves; apply 20g Zinc Sulfate per tree basin.
Chickoo (Sapota).11. Nitrogen Deficiency: General yellowing; apply 50kg compost per tree.
Chickoo (Sapota).12. Termites: Apply wood ash or Neem cake around the base.

Coriander (Dhaniya).1. Powdery Mildew: Dust with Sulfur powder or spray 10% milk-water.
Coriander (Dhaniya).2. Stem Gall: Use disease-free seeds; avoid late sowing.
Coriander (Dhaniya).3. Wilt: Practice crop rotation; treat seeds with Trichoderma.
Coriander (Dhaniya).4. Aphids: Spray Garlic-Chilli extract in the evening.
Coriander (Dhaniya).5. Thrips: Maintain high soil moisture; use blue sticky traps.
Coriander (Dhaniya).6. Root Rot: Improve drainage; avoid waterlogging in the field.
Coriander (Dhaniya).7. Damping Off: Avoid thick sowing; treat seeds with wood ash.
Coriander (Dhaniya).8. Leaf Spot: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture; ensure wide spacing.
Coriander (Dhaniya).9. Iron Deficiency: Top leaves turn yellow; spray Ferrous Sulfate.
Coriander (Dhaniya).10. Bacterial Blight: Soak seeds in 50°C water for 20 minutes.
Coriander (Dhaniya).11. Cutworms: Deep summer plowing to expose larvae to birds.
Coriander (Dhaniya).12. Mites: Spray forceful water or 2g/L Wettable Sulfur.

Plum (Aloobukhara).1. San Jose Scale: Spray dormant oil in winter; release parasitic wasps.
Plum (Aloobukhara).3. Leaf Curl: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture before bud burst in spring.
Plum (Aloobukhara).4. Plum Borer: Keep orchard clean; remove old bark from trunks.
Plum (Aloobukhara).5. Brown Rot: Remove and burn mummified fruits from the tree.
Plum (Aloobukhara).6. Bacterial Canker: Avoid pruning in winter; use copper-based paste.
Plum (Aloobukhara).8. Mites: Mist leaves with water; maintain plant vigor.
Plum (Aloobukhara).9. Root Rot: Ensure proper drainage; avoid clay-heavy soil.
Plum (Aloobukhara).10. Silver Leaf: Prune only in summer; paint wounds with copper paste.
Plum (Aloobukhara).11. Boron Deficiency: Fruit cracking; apply 5g Borax per tree.
Plum (Aloobukhara).12. Nitrogen Stress: Small yellow leaves; apply well-rotted manure.

Garlic (Lahsun).1. Purple Blotch: Spray mancozeb or 1% Bordeaux; crop rotation.
Garlic (Lahsun).2. Thrips: Use silver plastic mulch; spray water forcefully.
Garlic (Lahsun).3. Basal Rot: Treat cloves with Trichoderma; avoid field injury.
Garlic (Lahsun).4. Downy Mildew: Ensure wide spacing for airflow; avoid overhead water.
Garlic (Lahsun).5. Onion Maggot: Apply Neem cake to soil; rotate with non-bulb crops.
Garlic (Lahsun).6. Stemphylium Blight: Remove old crop debris; spray ginger-garlic extract.
Garlic (Lahsun).7. White Rot: Increase soil pH with lime; use clean seedlings.
Garlic (Lahsun).8. Neck Rot: Dry garlic thoroughly in sun before storage.
Garlic (Lahsun).9. Garlic Smut: Deep summer plowing; seed treatment with Thiram.
Garlic (Lahsun).10. Zinc Deficiency: Yellowing/twisting of leaves; apply Zinc Sulfate.
Garlic (Lahsun).11. Black Mold: Store in cool, dry, well-ventilated areas.
Garlic (Lahsun).12. Bulb Mite: Maintain humidity in storage; ensure proper drying.
